It's the function's responsibility to\n   111→        # call execute(sql, params, many, context).\n   112→        self.execute_wrappers = []\n   113→\n   114→        self.client = self.client_class(self)\n   115→        self.creation = self.creation_class(self)\n   116→        self.features = self.features_class(self)\n   117→        self.introspection = self.introspection_class(self)\n   118→        self.ops = self.ops_class(self)\n   119→        self.validation = self.validation_class(self)\n   120→\n   121→    def __repr__(self):\n   122→        return (\n   123→            f\"<{self.__class__.__qualname__} \"\n   124→            f\"vendor={self.vendor!r} alias={self.alias!r}>\"\n   125→        )\n   126→\n   127→    def ensure_timezone(self):\n   128→        \"\"\"\n   129→        Ensure the connection's timezone is set to `self.timezone_name` and\n   130→        return whether it changed or not.\n   131→        \"\"\"\n   132→        return False\n   133→\n   134→    @cached_property\n   135→    def timezone(self):\n   136→        \"\"\"\n   137→        Return a tzinfo of the database connection time zone.\n   138→\n   139→        This is only used when time zone support is enabled. When a datetime is\n   140→        read from the database, it is always returned in this time zone.\n   141→\n   142→        When the database backend supports time zones, it doesn't matter which\n   143→        time zone Django uses, as long as aware datetimes are used everywhere.\n   144→        Other users connecting to the database can choose their own time zone.\n   145→\n   146→        When the database backend doesn't support time zones, the time zone\n   147→        Django uses may be constrained by the requirements of other users of\n   148→        the database.\n   149→        \"\"\"\n   150→        if not settings.USE_TZ:\n   151→            return None\n   152→        elif self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"] is None:\n   153→            return datetime.UTC\n   154→        else:\n   155→            return zoneinfo.ZoneInfo(self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"])\n   156→\n   157→    @cached_property\n   158→    def timezone_name(self):\n   159→        \"\"\"\n   160→ self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"] is None:\n   165→            return \"UTC\"\n   166→        else:\n   167→            return self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"]\n   168→\n   169→    @property\n   170→    def queries_logged(self):\n   171→        return self.force_debug_cursor or settings.DEBUG\n   172→\n   173→    @property\n   174→    def queries(self):\n   175→        if len(self.queries_log) == self.queries_log.maxlen:\n   176→            warnings.warn(\n   177→                \"Limit for query logging exceeded, only the last {} queries \"\n   178→                \"will be returned.\".format(self.queries_log.maxlen),\n   179→                stacklevel=2,\n   180→            )\n   181→        return list(self.queries_log)\n   182→\n   183→    def get_database_version(self):\n   184→        \"\"\"Return a tuple of the database's version.\"\"\"\n   185→        raise NotImplementedError(\n   186→            \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a get_database_version() \"\n   187→            \"method.\"\n   188→        )\n   189→\n   190→    def check_database_version_supported(self):\n   191→        \"\"\"\n   192→        Raise an error if the database version isn't supported by this\n   193→        version of Django.\n   194→        \"\"\"\n   195→        if (\n   196→            self.features.minimum_database_version is not None\n   197→            and self.get_database_version() < self.features.minimum_database_version\n   198→        ):\n   199→            db_version = \".\".join(map(str, f\"{self.display_name} {min_db_version} or later is required \"\n   203→                f\"(found {db_version}).\"\n   204→            )\n   205→\n   206→    # ##### Backend-specific methods for creating connections and cursors #####\n   207→\n   208→    def get_connection_params(self):\n   209→        \"\"\"Return a dict of parameters suitable for get_new_connection.\"\"\"\n   210→ 227→\n   228→    def create_cursor(self, name=None):\n   229→        \"\"\"Create a cursor. Assume that the connection is closed.\"\"\"\n   239→        # Check for invalid configurations.\n   240→        self.check_settings()\n   241→        # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\n   242→        self.in_atomic_block = False\n   243→        self.savepoint_ids = []\n   244→        self.atomic_blocks = []\n   245→        self.needs_rollback = False\n   246→        # New connections are healthy.\n   253→        self.health_check_done = True\n   254→        # Establish the connection\n   255→        conn_params = self.get_connection_params()\n   256→        self.connection = self.get_new_connection(conn_params)\n   257→        self.set_autocommit(self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"])\n   258→        self.init_connection_state()\n   259→        connection_created.send(sender=self.__class__, connection=self)\n   260→\n   261→        self.run_on_commit = []\n   262→\n   263→    def check_settings(self):\n   264→        if self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"] is not None and not settings.USE_TZ:\n   265→            raise ImproperlyConfigured(\n   266→                \"Connection '%s' cannot set TIME_ZONE because USE_TZ is False.\"\n   267→                % self.alias\n   268→            )\n   269→\n   270→    @async_unsafe\n   271→    def ensure_connection(self):\n   272→        \"\"\"Guarantee that a connection to the database is established.\"\"\"\n   273→        if self.connection is None:\n   274→            if self.in_atomic_block and self.closed_in_transaction:\n   275→                raise ProgrammingError(\n   276→                    \"Cannot open a new connection in an atomic block.\"\n   277→                )\n   278→            with self.wrap_database_errors:\n   279→                self.connect()\n   280→\n   281→    # ##### Backend-specific wrappers for PEP-249 connection methods #####\n   282→\n   283→    def _prepare_cursor(self, cursor):\n   284→        \"\"\"\n   285→        Validate the connection is usable and perform database cursor wrapping.\n   286→        \"\"\"\n   287→        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   288→        if self.queries_logged:\n   289→            wrapped_cursor = self.make_debug_cursor(cursor)\n   290→        else:\n   291→            wrapped_cursor = self.make_cursor(cursor)\n   292→        return wrapped_cursor\n   293→\n   294→    def _cursor(self, name=None):\n   295→        self.close_if_health_check_failed()\n   296→        self.ensure_connection()\n   297→        with self.wrap_database_errors:\n   298→            return self._prepare_cursor(self.create_cursor(name))\n   299→\n   300→    def _commit(self):\n   301→        if self.connection is not None:\n   302→            with debug_transaction(self, \"COMMIT\"), self.wrap_database_errors:\n   303→                return self.connection.commit()\n   304→\n   305→    def _rollback(self):\n   306→        if self.connection is not None:\n   307→            with debug_transaction(self, \"ROLLBACK\"), self.wrap_database_errors:\n   308→                return self.connection.rollback()\n   309→\n   310→    def _close(self):\n   311→        if self.connection is not None:\n   312→            with self.wrap_database_errors:\n   313→                return self.connection.close()\n   314→\n   315→    # ##### Generic wrappers for PEP-249 connection methods #####\n   316→\n   317→    @async_unsafe\n   318→    def cursor(self):\n   319→        \"\"\"Create a cursor, opening a connection if necessary.\"\"\"\n   320→        return self._cursor()\n   321→\n   322→    @async_unsafe\n   323→    def commit(self):\n   324→        \"\"\"Commit a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\"\"\"\n   325→        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   326→        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n   327→        self._commit()\n   328→        # A successful commit means that the database connection works.\n   329→        self.errors_occurred = False\n   330→        self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = True\n   331→\n   332→    @async_unsafe\n   333→    def rollback(self):\n   334→        \"\"\"Roll back a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\"\"\"\n   335→        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   336→        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n   337→        self._rollback()\n   338→        # A successful rollback means that the database connection works.\n   339→        self.errors_occurred = False\n   340→        self.needs_rollback = False\n   341→        self.run_on_commit = []\n   342→\n   343→    @async_unsafe\n   344→    def close(self):\n   345→        \"\"\"Close the connection to the database.\"\"\"\n   346→        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   347→        self.run_on_commit = []\n   348→\n   349→        # Don't call validate_no_atomic_block() to avoid making it difficult\n   350→        # to get rid of a connection in an invalid state. The next connect()\n   351→        # will reset the transaction state anyway.\n   352→        if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\n   353→            return\n   354→        try:\n   355→            self._close()\n   356→        finally:\n   357→            if self.in_atomic_block:\n   358→                self.closed_in_transaction = True\n   359→                self.needs_rollback = True\n   360→            else:\n   361→                self.connection = None\n   362→\n   363→    # ##### Backend-specific savepoint management methods #####\n   364→\n   365→    def _savepoint(self, sid):\n   366→        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n   367→            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_create_sql(sid))\n   368→\n   369→    def _savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n   370→        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n   371→            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_rollback_sql(sid))\n   372→\n   373→    def _savepoint_commit(self, sid):\n   374→        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n   375→            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_commit_sql(sid))\n   376→\n   377→    def _savepoint_allowed(self):\n   378→        # Savepoints cannot be created outside a transaction\n   379→        return self.features.uses_savepoints and not self.get_autocommit()\n   380→\n   381→    # ##### Generic savepoint management methods #####\n   382→\n   383→    @async_unsafe\n   384→    def savepoint(self):\n   385→        \"\"\"\n   386→        Create a savepoint inside the current transaction. Return an\n   387→        identifier for the savepoint that will be used for the subsequent\n   388→        rollback or commit. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n   389→        \"\"\"\n   390→        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n   391→            return\n   392→\n   393→        thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\n   394→        tid = str(thread_ident).replace(\"-\", \"\")\n   395→\n   396→        self.savepoint_state += 1\n   397→        sid = \"s%s_x%d\" % (tid, self.savepoint_state)\n   398→\n   399→        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   400→        self._savepoint(sid)\n   401→\n   402→        return sid\n   403→\n   404→    @async_unsafe\n   405→    def savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n   406→        \"\"\"\n   407→        Roll back to a savepoint. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n   408→        \"\"\"\n   409→        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n   410→            return\n   411→\n   412→        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   413→        self._savepoint_rollback(sid)\n   414→\n   415→        # Remove any callbacks registered while this savepoint was active.\n   416→        self.run_on_commit = [\n   417→            (sids, func, robust)\n   418→            for (sids, func, robust) in self.run_on_commit\n   419→            if sid not in sids\n   420→        ]\n   421→\n   422→    @async_unsafe\n   423→    def savepoint_commit(self, sid):\n   424→        \"\"\"\n   425→        Release a savepoint. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n   426→        \"\"\"\n   427→        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n   428→            return\n   429→\n   430→        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   431→        self._savepoint_commit(sid)\n   432→\n   433→    @async_unsafe\n   434→    def clean_savepoints(self):\n   435→        \"\"\"\n   436→        Reset the counter used to generate unique savepoint ids in this thread.\n   437→        \"\"\"\n   438→        self.savepoint_state = 0\n   439→\n   440→    # ##### Backend-specific transaction management methods #####\n   441→\n   442→    def _set_autocommit(self, autocommit):\n   443→        \"\"\"\n   444→        Backend-specific implementation to enable or disable autocommit.\n   445→        \"\"\"\n   446→        raise NotImplementedError(\n   447→            \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a _set_autocommit() method\"\n   448→        )\n   449→\n   450→    # ##### Generic transaction management methods #####\n   451→\n   452→    def get_autocommit(self):\n   453→        \"\"\"Get the autocommit state.\"\"\"\n   454→        self.ensure_connection()\n   455→        return self.autocommit\n   456→\n   457→    def set_autocommit(\n   458→        self, autocommit, force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=False\n   459→    ):\n   460→        \"\"\"\n   461→        Enable or disable autocommit.\n   462→\n   463→        The usual way to start a transaction is to turn autocommit off.\n   464→        SQLite does not properly start a transaction when disabling\n   465→        autocommit. To avoid this buggy behavior and to actually enter a new\n   466→        transaction, an explicit BEGIN is required. Using\n   467→        force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=True will issue an\n   468→        explicit BEGIN with SQLite. This option will be ignored for other\n   469→        backends.\n   470→        \"\"\"\n   471→        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n   472→        self.close_if_health_check_failed()\n   473→        self.ensure_connection()\n   474→\n   475→        start_transaction_under_autocommit = (\n   476→            force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit\n   477→            and not autocommit\n   478→            and hasattr(self, \"_start_transaction_under_autocommit\")\n   479→        )\n   480→\n   481→        if start_transaction_under_autocommit:\n   482→            self._start_transaction_under_autocommit()\n   483→        elif autocommit:\n   484→            self._set_autocommit(autocommit)\n   485→        else:\n   486→            with debug_transaction(self, \"BEGIN\"):\n   487→                self._set_autocommit(autocommit)\n   488→        self.autocommit = autocommit\n   489→\n   490→        if autocommit and get_rollback(self):\n   495→        \"\"\"Get the \"needs rollback\" flag -- for *advanced use* only.\"\"\"\n   496→        if not self.in_atomic_block:\n   497→            raise TransactionManagementError(\n   498→                \"The rollback flag doesn't work outside of an 'atomic' block.\"\n   499→            )\n   500→        return self.needs_rollback\n   501→\n   502→    def set_rollback(self, rollback):\n   503→        \"\"\"\n   504→        Set or unset the \"needs rollback\" flag -- for *advanced use* only.\n   505→        \"\"\"\n   506→        if not self.in_atomic_block:\n   507→            raise TransactionManagementError(\n   508→                \"The rollback flag doesn't work outside of an 'atomic' block.\"\n   509→            )\n   510→        self.needs_rollback = rollback\n   511→\n   512→    def validate_no_atomic_block(self):\n   513→        \"\"\"Raise an error if an atomic block is active.\"\"\"\n   514→        if self.in_atomic_block:\n   515→            raise TransactionManagementError(\n   516→                \"This is forbidden when an 'atomic' block is active.\"\n   517→            )\n   518→\n   519→    def validate_no_broken_transaction(self):\n   520→        if self.needs_rollback:\n   521→            raise TransactionManagementError(\n   522→                \"An error occurred in the current transaction. You can't \"\n   523→                \"execute queries until the end of the 'atomic' block.\"\n   524→            ) from self.rollback_exc\n   525→\n   526→    # ##### Foreign key constraints checks handling #####\n   527→\n   528→    @contextmanager\n   529→    def constraint_checks_disabled(self):\n   530→        \"\"\"\n   531→        Disable foreign key constraint checking.\n   532→        \"\"\"\n   533→        disabled = self.disable_constraint_checking()\n   534→        try:\n   535→            yield\n   536→        finally:\n   537→            if disabled:\n   538→                self.enable_constraint_checking()\n   539→\n   540→    def disable_constraint_checking(self):\n   541→        \"\"\"\n   542→        Backends can implement as needed to temporarily disable foreign key\n   543→        constraint checking. Should raise an\n   559→        IntegrityError if any invalid foreign key references are encountered.\n   560→        \"\"\"\n   561→        pass\n   562→\n   563→    # ##### Connection termination handling #####\n   564→\n   565→    def is_usable(self):\n   566→        \"\"\"\n   567→        Test if the database connection is usable.\n   568→\n   569→        This method may assume that self.connection is not None.\n   570→\n   571→        Actual implementations should take care not to raise exceptions\n   572→        as that may prevent Django from recycling unusable connections.\n   573→        \"\"\"\n   574→        raise NotImplementedError(\n   575→            \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require an is_usable() method\"\n   576→        )\n   577→\n   578→    def close_if_health_check_failed(self):\n   579→        \"\"\"Close existing connection if it fails a health check.\"\"\"\n   580→        if (\n   581→            self.connection is None\n   582→            or not self.health_check_enabled\n   583→            or self.health_check_done\n   584→        ):\n   585→            return\n   586→\n   587→        if not self.is_usable():\n   588→            self.close()\n   589→        self.health_check_done = True\n   590→\n   591→    def close_if_unusable_or_obsolete(self):\n   592→        \"\"\"\n   593→        Close the current connection if unrecoverable errors have occurred\n   594→        or if it outlived its maximum age.\n   595→        \"\"\"\n   596→        if self.connection is not None:\n   597→            self.health_check_done = False\n   598→            # If the application didn't restore the original autocommit\n   599→            # setting, don't take chances, drop the connection.\n   600→            if self.get_autocommit() != self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"]:\n   601→                self.close()\n   602→                return\n   603→\n   604→            # If an exception other than DataError or IntegrityError occurred\n   605→            # since the last commit / rollback, check if the connection works.\n   606→            if self.errors_occurred:\n   607→                if self.is_usable():\n   608→                    self.errors_occurred = False\n   609→                    self.health_check_done = True\n   610→                else:\n   611→                    self.close()\n   612→                    return\n   613→\n   614→            if self.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= self.close_at:\n   615→                self.close()\n   616→                return\n   617→\n   618→    # ##### Thread safety handling #####\n   619→\n   620→    @property\n   621→    def allow_thread_sharing(self):\n   622→        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n   623→            return self._thread_sharing_count > 0\n   624→\n   625→    def inc_thread_sharing(self):\n   626→        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n   627→            self._thread_sharing_count += 1\n   628→\n   629→    def dec_thread_sharing(self):\n   630→        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n   631→            if self._thread_sharing_count <= 0:\n   632→                raise RuntimeError(\n   633→                    \"Cannot decrement the thread sharing count below zero.\"\n   634→                )\n   635→            self._thread_sharing_count -= 1\n   636→\n   637→    def validate_thread_sharing(self):\n   638→        \"\"\"\n   639→        Validate that the connection isn't accessed by another thread than the\n   640→        one which originally created it, unless the connection was explicitly\n   641→        authorized to be shared between threads (via the `inc_thread_sharing()`\n   642→        method). Raise an exception if the validation fails.\n   643→        \"\"\"\n   644→        if not (self.allow_thread_sharing or self._thread_ident == _thread.get_ident()):\n   645→            raise DatabaseError(\n   646→                \"DatabaseWrapper objects created in a \"\n   647→                \"thread can only be used in that same thread. The object \"\n   648→                \"with alias '%s' was created in thread id %s and this is \"\n   649→                \"thread id %s.\" % (self.alias, self._thread_ident, _thread.get_ident())\n   650→            )\n   651→\n   652→    # ##### Miscellaneous #####\n   653→\n   654→    def prepare_database(self):\n   655→        \"\"\"\n   656→        Hook to do any database check or preparation, generally called before\n   657→        migrating a project or an app.\n   658→        \"\"\"\n   659→        pass\n   660→\n   661→    @cached_property\n   662→    def wrap_database_errors(self):\n   663→        \"\"\"\n   664→        Context manager and decorator that re-throws backend-specific database\n   665→        exceptions using Django's common wrappers.\n   666→        \"\"\"\n   667→        return DatabaseErrorWrapper(self)\n   668→\n   669→    def chunked_cursor(self):\n   670→        \"\"\"\n   671→        Return a cursor that tries to avoid caching in the database (if\n   672→        supported by the database), otherwise return a regular cursor.\n   673→        \"\"\"\n   674→        return self.cursor()\n   675→\n   676→    def make_debug_cursor(self, cursor):\n   677→        \"\"\"Create a cursor that logs all queries in self.queries_log.\"\"\"\n   678→        return utils.CursorDebugWrapper(cursor, self)\n   679→\n   680→    def make_cursor(self, cursor):\n   681→        \"\"\"Create a cursor without debug logging.\"\"\"\n   682→        return utils.CursorWrapper(cursor, self)\n   683→\n   684→    @contextmanager\n   685→    def temporary_connection(self):\n   686→        \"\"\"\n   687→        Context manager that ensures that a connection is established, and\n   688→        if it opened one, closes it to avoid leaving a dangling connection.\n   689→        This is useful for operations outside of the request-response cycle.\n   690→\n   691→        Provide a cursor: with self.temporary_connection() as cursor: ...\n   692→        \"\"\"\n   693→        must_close = self.connection is None\n   694→        try:\n   695→            with self.cursor() as cursor:\n   696→                yield cursor\n   697→        finally:\n   698→            if must_close:\n   699→                self.close()\n   700→\n   701→    @contextmanager\n   702→    def _nodb_cursor(self):\n   703→        \"\"\"\n   704→        Return a cursor from an alternative connection to be used when there is\n   705→        no need to access the main database, specifically for test db\n   706→        creation/deletion. This also prevents the production database from\n   707→        being exposed to potential child threads while (or after) the test\n   708→        database is destroyed. Assume that the connection is closed.\"\"\"\n        # Check for invalid configurations.\n        self.check_settings()\n        # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\n        self.in_atomic_block = False\n        self.savepoint_ids = []\n        self.atomic_blocks = []\n        self.needs_rollback = False\n        # New connections are healthy.\n        self.health_check_done = True\n        # Establish the connection\n        conn_params = self.get_connection_params()\n        self.connection = self.get_new_connection(conn_params)\n        self.set_autocommit(self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"])\n        self.init_connection_state()\n        connection_created.send(sender=self.__class__, connection=self)\n\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n    def check_settings(self):\n        if self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"] is not None and not settings.USE_TZ:\n            raise ImproperlyConfigured(\n                \"Connection '%s' cannot set TIME_ZONE because USE_TZ is False.\"\n                % self.alias\n            )\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def ensure_connection(self):\n        \"\"\"Guarantee that a connection to the database is established.\"\"\"\n        if self.connection is None:\n            if self.in_atomic_block and self.closed_in_transaction:\n                raise ProgrammingError(\n                    \"Cannot open a new connection in an atomic block.\"\n                )\n            with self.wrap_database_errors:\n                self.connect()\n\n    # A successful commit means that the database connection works.\n        self.errors_occurred = False\n        self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = True\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def rollback(self):\n        \"\"\"Roll back a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        self._rollback()\n        # A successful rollback means that the database connection works.\n        self.errors_occurred = False\n        self.needs_rollback = False\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close the connection to the database.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n        # The next connect()\n        # will reset the transaction state anyway.\n        if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\n            return\n        try:\n            self._close()\n        finally:\n            if self.in_atomic_block:\n                self.closed_in_transaction = True\n                self.needs_rollback = True\n            else:\n                self.connection = None\n\n    # ##### Backend-specific savepoint management methods #####\n\n    def _savepoint(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_create_sql(sid))\n\n    def _savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_rollback_sql(sid))\n\n    def _savepoint_commit(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_commit_sql(sid))\n\n    def _savepoint_allowed(self):\n        # Savepoints cannot be created outside a transaction\n        return self.features.uses_savepoints and not self.get_autocommit()\n\n    # ##### Generic savepoint management methods #####\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a savepoint inside the current transaction. Return an\n        identifier for the savepoint that will be used for the subsequent\n        rollback or commit. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\n        tid = str(thread_ident).replace(\"-\", \"\")\n\n        self.savepoint_state += 1\n        sid = \"s%s_x%d\" % (tid, self.savepoint_state)\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint(sid)\n\n        return sid\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n        \"\"\"\n        Roll back to a savepoint. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint_rollback(sid)\n\n        # Remove any callbacks registered while this savepoint was active.\n        self.run_on_commit = [\n            (sids, func, robust)\n            for (sids, func, robust) in self.run_on_commit\n            if sid not in sids\n        ]\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint_commit(self, sid):\n        \"\"\"\n        Release a savepoint. To avoid this buggy behavior and to actually enter a new\n        transaction, an explicit BEGIN is required. Using\n        force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=True will issue an\n        explicit BEGIN with SQLite. This option will be ignored for other\n        backends.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        self.close_if_health_check_failed()\n        self.ensure_connection()\n\n   \"The rollback flag doesn't work outside of an 'atomic' block.\"\n            )\n        return self.needs_rollback\n\n    def set_rollback(self, rollback):\n        \"\"\"\n        Set or unset the work outside of an 'atomic' block.\"\n            )\n        self.needs_rollback = rollback\n\n    def validate_no_atomic_block(self):\n        \"\"\"Raise an error if an atomic block is active.\"\"\"\n        if self.in_atomic_block:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"This is forbidden when an 'atomic' block is active.\"\n            )\n\n    def validate_no_broken_transaction(self):\n        if self.needs_rollback:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"An error occurred in the current transaction. You can't \"\n                \"execute queries until the end of the 'atomic' block.\"\n            ) from self.rollback_exc\n\n    # ##### Foreign key constraints checks handling #####\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def constraint_checks_disabled(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Disable foreign key constraint checking.\n        \"\"\"\n        disabled = self.disable_constraint_checking()\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            if disabled:\n                self.enable_constraint_checking()\n\n    def disable_constraint_checking(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Backends can implement as needed to temporarily disable foreign key\n        constraint checking. If the application didn't restore the original autocommit\n            # setting, don't take chances, drop the connection.\n            if self.get_autocommit() != self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"]:\n                self.close()\n                return\n\n            # If an exception other than DataError or IntegrityError occurred\n            # since the last commit / rollback, check if the connection works.\n            if self.errors_occurred:\n                if self.is_usable():\n                    self.errors_occurred = False\n                    self.health_check_done = True\n                else:\n                    self.close()\n                    return\n\n            if self.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= self.close_at:\n                self.close()\n                return\n\n    # ##### Thread safety handling #####\n\n    @property\n    def allow_thread_sharing(self):\n        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n            return self._thread_sharing_count > 0\n\n    def inc_thread_sharing(self):\n        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n            self._thread_sharing_count += 1\n\n    def dec_thread_sharing(self):\n        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n            if self._thread_sharing_count <= 0:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"Cannot decrement the thread sharing count below zero.\"\n                )\n            self._thread_sharing_count -= 1\n\n    def validate_thread_sharing(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate that the connection isn't accessed by another thread than the\n        one which originally created it, unless the connection was explicitly\n        authorized to be shared between threads (via the `inc_thread_sharing()`\n        method). Raise an exception if the validation fails.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not (self.allow_thread_sharing or self._thread_ident == _thread.get_ident()):\n            raise DatabaseError(\n                \"DatabaseWrapper objects created in a \"\n                \"thread can only be used in that same thread. The object \"\n                \"with alias '%s' was created in thread id %s and this is \"\n                \"thread id %s.\" % (self.alias, self._thread_ident, _thread.get_ident())\n            )\n\n    # ##### Miscellaneous #####\n\n    def prepare_database(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Hook to do any database check or preparation, generally called before\n        migrating a project or an app.\n        \"\"\"\n        pass\n\n    @cached_property\n    def wrap_database_errors(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Context manager and decorator that re-throws backend-specific database\n        exceptions using Django's common wrappers.\n        \"\"\"\n        return DatabaseErrorWrapper(self)\n\n    def chunked_cursor(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a cursor that tries to avoid caching in the database (if\n        supported by the database), otherwise return a regular cursor.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.cursor()\n\n    def make_debug_cursor(self, cursor):\n        \"\"\"Create a cursor that logs all queries in self.queries_log.\"\"\"\n        return utils.CursorDebugWrapper(cursor, self)\n\n    def make_cursor(self, cursor):\n        \"\"\"Create a cursor without debug logging.\"\"\"\n        return utils.CursorWrapper(cursor, self)\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def temporary_connection(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Context manager that ensures that a connection is established, and\n        if it opened one, closes it to avoid leaving a dangling connection.\n        This is useful for operations outside of the request-response cycle.\n\n        Provide a cursor: with self.temporary_connection() as cursor: ...\n        \"\"\"\n        must_close = self.connection is None\n        try:\n            with self.cursor() as cursor:\n                yield cursor\n        finally:\n            if must_close:\n                self.close()\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def _nodb_cursor(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a cursor from an alternative connection to be used when there is\n        no need to access the main database, specifically for test db\n        creation/deletion. This also prevents the production database from\n        being exposed to potential child threads while (or after) the test\n        database is destroyed. Refs #10868, #17786, #16969.\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self.__class__({**self.settings_dict, \"NAME\": None}, alias=NO_DB_ALIAS)\n        try:\n            with conn.cursor() as cursor:\n                yield cursor\n        finally:\n            conn.close()\n\n    def schema_editor(self, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a new instance of this backend's SchemaEditor.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.SchemaEditorClass is None:\n            raise NotImplementedError(\n                \"The SchemaEditorClass attribute of this database wrapper is still None\"\n            )\n        return self.SchemaEditorClass(self, *args, **kwargs)\n\n    def on_commit(self, func, robust=False):\n        if not callable(func):\n            raise TypeError(\"on_commit()'s callback must be a callable.\")\n        if self.in_atomic_block:\n            # Transaction in progress; save for execution on commit.\n            self.run_on_commit.append((set(self.savepoint_ids), func, robust))\n        elif not self.get_autocommit():\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"on_commit() cannot be used in manual transaction management\"\n            )\n        else:\n            # No transaction in progress and in autocommit mode; execute\n            # immediately.\n            if robust:\n                try:\n                    func()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    name = getattr(func, \"__qualname__\", func)\n                    logger.exception(\"Error calling %s in on_commit() (%s).\", name, e)\n            else:\n                func()\n\n    def run_and_clear_commit_hooks(self):\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        current_run_on_commit = self.run_on_commit\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n        while current_run_on_commit:\n            _, func, robust = current_run_on_commit.pop(0)\n            if robust:\n                try:\n                    func()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    name = getattr(func, \"__qualname__\", func)\n                    logger.exception(\n                        \"Error calling %s in on_commit() during transaction (%s).\",\n                        name,\n                        e,\n                    )\n            else:\n                func()\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def execute_wrapper(self, wrapper):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a context manager under which the wrapper is applied to suitable\n        database query executions.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.execute_wrappers.append(wrapper)\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            self.execute_wrappers.pop()\n\n    def copy(self, alias=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a copy of this connection.\n\n        For tests that require two connections to the same database.\n        \"\"\"\n        settings_dict = copy.deepcopy(self.settings_dict)\n        if alias is None:\n            alias = self.alias\n        return type(self)(settings_dict, alias)\n","numLines":790,"startLine":1,"totalLines":790}}}

are considered healthy without performing\n   289→        # the health check.\n   290→        with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n   291→            self.run_query()\n   292→\n   293→        old_connection = connection.connection\n   294→        # Simulate request_finished.\n   295→        connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n   296→ \"is_usable\", return_value=False\n   301→        ) as mocked_is_usable:\n   302→            self.run_query()\n   303→            new_connection = connection.connection\n   304→            # A new connection is established.\n   305→            self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)\n   306→            # Only one health check per \"request\" is performed, so the next\n   307→            # query will carry on even if the health check fails. Next query\n   308→            # succeeds because the real connection is healthy and only the\n   309→            # health check failure is mocked.\n   310→            self.run_query()\n   311→            self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\n   312→        self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)\n   313→\n   314→        # self.addCleanup(gc.set_debug, 0)\n        self.addCleanup(gc.enable)\n\n        # Disable automatic garbage collection to control when it's triggered,\n        # then run a full collection cycle to ensure `gc.garbage` is empty.\n        gc.disable()\n        gc.collect()\n\n        # The garbage list isn't automatically populated to avoid CPU overhead,\n        # so debugging needs to be enabled to track all unreachable items and\n        # have them stored in `gc.garbage`.\n        gc.set_debug(gc.DEBUG_SAVEALL)\n\n        # Create a new connection that will be closed during the test, and When a datetime is\n        read from the database, it is always returned in this time zone.\n\n        When the database backend supports time zones, it doesn't matter which\n        time zone Django uses, as long as aware datetimes are used everywhere.\n        Other users connecting to the database can choose their own time zone.\n\n        When the database backend doesn't support time zones, the time zone\n        Django uses may be constrained by the requirements of other users of\n        the database.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not settings.USE_TZ:\n            return None\n     \"\"\"Create a cursor. Assume that the connection is closed.\"\"\"\n        # Check for invalid configurations.\n        self.check_settings()\n        # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\n        self.in_atomic_block = False\n        self.savepoint_ids = []\n        self.atomic_blocks = []\n        self.needs_rollback = False\n        # New connections are healthy.\n        self.health_check_done = True\n        # Establish the connection\n        conn_params = self.get_connection_params()\n        self.connection = self.get_new_connection(conn_params)\n        self.set_autocommit(self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"])\n        self.init_connection_state()\n        connection_created.send(sender=self.__class__, connection=self)\n\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n    def check_settings(self):\n        if self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"] is not None and not settings.USE_TZ:\n            raise ImproperlyConfigured(\n                \"Connection '%s' cannot set TIME_ZONE because USE_TZ is False.\"\n                % self.alias\n            )\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def ensure_connection(self):\n        \"\"\"Guarantee that a connection to the database is established.\"\"\"\n        if self.connection is None:\n            if self.in_atomic_block and self.closed_in_transaction:\n                raise ProgrammingError(\n                    \"Cannot open a new connection in an atomic block.\"\n                )\n            with self.wrap_database_errors:\n                self.connect()\n\n    #     \"\"\"Create a cursor. Assume that the connection is closed.\"\"\"\n        # Check for invalid configurations.\n        self.check_settings()\n        # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\n        self.in_atomic_block = False\n        self.savepoint_ids = []\n        self.atomic_blocks = []\n        self.needs_rollback = False\n        # Reset parameters defining when to close/health-check the connection.\n        self.health_check_enabled = self.settings_dict[\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\"]\n        max_age = self.settings_dict[\"CONN_MAX_AGE\"]\n        self.close_at = None if max_age != 0 else time.monotonic() + max_age\n        self.closed_in_transaction = False\n        self.errors_occurred = False\n        # New connections are healthy.\n        self.health_check_done = True\n        # Establish the connection\n        conn_params = self.get_connection_params()\n        self.connection = self.get_new_connection(conn_params)\n        self.set_autocommit(self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"])\n        self.init_connection_state()\n        connection_created.send(sender=self.__class__, connection=self)\n\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n    def check_settings(self):\n        if self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"] is not None and not settings.USE_TZ:\n            raise ImproperlyConfigured(\n                \"Connection '%s' cannot set TIME_ZONE because USE_TZ is False.\"\n                % self.alias\n            )\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def ensure_connection(self):\n        \"\"\"Guarantee that a connection to the database is established.\"\"\"\n        if self.connection is None:\n            if self.in_atomic_block and self.closed_in_transaction:\n                raise ProgrammingError(\n                    \"Cannot open a new connection in an atomic block.\"\n                )\n            with self.wrap_database_errors:\n                self.connect()\n\n    # ##### Generic wrappers for PEP-249 connection methods #####\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def cursor(self):\n        \"\"\"Create a cursor, opening a connection if necessary.\"\"\"\n        return self._cursor()\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def commit(self):\n        \"\"\"Commit a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        self._commit()\n        # A successful commit means that the database connection works.\n        self.errors_occurred = False\n        self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = True\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def rollback(self):\n        \"\"\"Roll back a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        self._rollback()\n        # A successful rollback means that the database connection works.\n        self.errors_occurred = False\n        self.needs_rollback = False\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close the connection to the database.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n        # Don't call validate_no_atomic_block() to avoid making it difficult\n        # to get rid of a connection in an invalid state. The next connect()\n        # will reset the transaction state anyway.\n        if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\n            return\n        try:\n            self._close()\n        finally:\n            if self.in_atomic_block:\n                self.closed_in_transaction = True\n                self.needs_rollback = True\n            else:\n                self.connection = None\n\n    # ##### Backend-specific savepoint management methods #####\n\n    def _savepoint(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_create_sql(sid))\n\n    def _savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_rollback_sql(sid))\n\n    def _savepoint_commit(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_commit_sql(sid))\n\n    def _savepoint_allowed(self):\n        # Savepoints cannot be created outside a transaction\n        return self.features.uses_savepoints and not self.get_autocommit()\n\n    # ##### Generic savepoint management methods #####\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a savepoint inside the current transaction. Return an\n        identifier for the savepoint that will be used for the subsequent\n        rollback or commit. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\n        tid = str(thread_ident).replace(\"-\", \"\")\n\n        self.savepoint_state += 1\n        sid = \"s%s_x%d\" % (tid, self.savepoint_state)\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint(sid)\n\n        return sid\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n        \"\"\"\n        Roll back to a savepoint. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint_rollback(sid)\n\n        # Remove any callbacks registered while this savepoint was active.\n        self.run_on_commit = [\n            (sids, func, robust)\n            for (sids, func, robust) in self.run_on_commit\n            if sid not in sids\n        ]\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint_commit(self, sid):\n        \"\"\"\n        Release a savepoint. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint_commit(sid)\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def clean_savepoints(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Reset the counter used to generate unique savepoint ids in this thread.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.savepoint_state = 0\n\n    # ##### Backend-specific transaction management methods #####\n\n    def _set_autocommit(self, autocommit):\n        \"\"\"\n        Backend-specific implementation to enable or disable autocommit.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError(\n            \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a _set_autocommit() method\"\n        )\n\n    # ##### Generic transaction management methods #####\n\n    def get_autocommit(self):\n        \"\"\"Get the autocommit state.\"\"\"\n        self.ensure_connection()\n        return self.autocommit\n\n    def set_autocommit(\n        self, autocommit, force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=False\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Enable or disable autocommit.\n\n        The usual way to start a transaction is to turn autocommit off.\n        SQLite does not properly start a transaction when disabling\n        autocommit. To avoid this buggy behavior and to actually enter a new\n        transaction, an explicit BEGIN is required. Using\n        force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=True will issue an\n        explicit BEGIN with SQLite. This option will be ignored for other\n        backends.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        self.close_if_health_check_failed()\n        self.ensure_connection()\n\n \"_start_transaction_under_autocommit\")\n        )\n\n        if start_transaction_under_autocommit:\n            self._start_transaction_under_autocommit()\n        elif autocommit:\n            self._set_autocommit(autocommit)\n        else:\n            with debug_transaction(self, \"BEGIN\"):\n                self._set_autocommit(autocommit)\n        self.autocommit = autocommit\n\n        if autocommit and self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on:\n            self.run_and_clear_commit_hooks()\n            self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = False\n\n    def get_rollback(self):\n        \"\"\"Get the \"needs rollback\" flag -- for *advanced use* only.\"\"\"\n        if not self.in_atomic_block:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"The rollback flag doesn't work outside of an 'atomic' block.\"\n            )\n        return self.needs_rollback\n\n    def set_rollback(self, rollback):\n        \"\"\"\n        Set or unset the \"needs rollback\" flag -- for *advanced use* only.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.in_atomic_block:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"The rollback flag doesn't work outside of an 'atomic' block.\"\n            )\n        self.needs_rollback = rollback\n\n    def validate_no_atomic_block(self):\n        \"\"\"Raise an error if an atomic block is active.\"\"\"\n        if self.in_atomic_block:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"This is forbidden when an 'atomic' block is active.\"\n            )\n\n    def validate_no_broken_transaction(self):\n        if self.needs_rollback:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"An error occurred in the current transaction. You can't \"\n                \"execute queries until the end of the 'atomic' block.\"\n            ) from self.rollback_exc\n\n    # ##### Foreign key constraints checks handling #####\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def constraint_checks_disabled(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Disable foreign key constraint checking.\n        \"\"\"\n        disabled = self.disable_constraint_checking()\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            if disabled:\n                self.enable_constraint_checking()\n\n    def disable_constraint_checking(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Backends can implement as needed to temporarily disable foreign key\n        constraint checking. Should return True if the constraints were\n        disabled and will need to be reenabled.\n        \"\"\"\n        return False\n\n    def enable_constraint_checking(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Backends can implement as needed to re-enable foreign key constraint\n        checking.\n        \"\"\"\n        pass\n\n    def check_constraints(self, table_names=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Backends can override this method if they can apply constraint\n        checking (e.g. via \"SET CONSTRAINTS ALL IMMEDIATE\"). Should raise an\n        IntegrityError if any invalid foreign key references are encountered.\n        \"\"\"\n        pass\n\n    # ##### Connection termination handling #####\n\n    def is_usable(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test if the database connection is usable.\n\n        This method may assume that self.connection is not None.\n\n        Actual implementations should take care not to raise exceptions\n        as that may prevent Django from recycling unusable connections.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError(\n            \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require an is_usable() method\"\n        )\n\n    def close_if_health_check_failed(self):\n        \"\"\"Close existing connection if it fails a health check.\"\"\"\n        if (\n            self.connection is None\n            or not self.health_check_enabled\n            or self.health_check_done\n        ):\n            return\n\n        if not self.is_usable():\n            self.close()\n        self.health_check_done = True\n\n    def close_if_unusable_or_obsolete(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Close the current connection if unrecoverable errors have occurred\n        or if it outlived its maximum age.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.connection is not None:\n            self.health_check_done = False\n            # If the application didn't restore the original autocommit\n            # setting, don't take chances, drop the connection.\n            if self.get_autocommit() != self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"]:\n                self.close()\n                return\n\n            # If an exception other than DataError or IntegrityError occurred\n            # since the last commit / rollback, check if the connection works.\n            if self.errors_occurred:\n                if self.is_usable():\n                    self.errors_occurred = False\n                    self.health_check_done = True\n                else:\n                    self.close()\n                    return\n\n            if self.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= self.close_at:\n                self.close()\n                return\n\n    # ##### Thread safety handling #####\n\n    @property\n    def allow_thread_sharing(self):\n        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n            return self._thread_sharing_count > 0\n\n    def inc_thread_sharing(self):\n        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n            self._thread_sharing_count += 1\n\n    def dec_thread_sharing(self):\n        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n            if self._thread_sharing_count <= 0:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"Cannot decrement the thread sharing count below zero.\"\n                )\n            self._thread_sharing_count -= 1\n\n    def validate_thread_sharing(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate that the connection isn't accessed by another thread than the\n        one which originally created it, unless the connection was explicitly\n        authorized to be shared between threads (via the `inc_thread_sharing()`\n        method). Raise an exception if the validation fails.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not (self.allow_thread_sharing or self._thread_ident == _thread.get_ident()):\n            raise DatabaseError(\n                \"DatabaseWrapper objects created in a \"\n                \"thread can only be used in that same thread. The object \"\n                \"with alias '%s' was created in thread id %s and this is \"\n                \"thread id %s.\" % (self.alias, self._thread_ident, _thread.get_ident())\n            )\n\n    # This also prevents the production database from\n        being exposed to potential child threads while (or after) the test\n        database is destroyed. Refs #10868, #17786, #16969.\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self.__class__({**self.settings_dict, \"NAME\": None}, alias=NO_DB_ALIAS)\n        try:\n            with conn.cursor() as cursor:\n                yield cursor\n        finally:\n            conn.close()\n\n    def schema_editor(self, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a new instance of this backend's SchemaEditor.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.SchemaEditorClass is None:\n            raise NotImplementedError(\n                \"The SchemaEditorClass attribute of this database wrapper is still None\"\n            )\n        return self.SchemaEditorClass(self, *args, **kwargs)\n\n    def on_commit(self, func, robust=False):\n        if not callable(func):\n            raise TypeError(\"on_commit()'s callback must be a callable.\")\n        if self.in_atomic_block:\n            # Transaction in progress; save for execution on commit.\n            self.run_on_commit.append((set(self.savepoint_ids), func, robust))\n        elif not self.get_autocommit():\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"on_commit() cannot be used in manual transaction management\"\n            )\n        else:\n            # No transaction in progress and in autocommit mode; execute\n            # immediately.\n            if robust:\n                try:\n                    func()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    name = getattr(func, \"__qualname__\", func)\n                    logger.exception(\"Error calling %s in on_commit() (%s).\", name, e)\n            else:\n                func()\n\n    def run_and_clear_commit_hooks(self):\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        current_run_on_commit = self.run_on_commit\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n        while current_run_on_commit:\n            _, func, robust = current_run_on_commit.pop(0)\n            if robust:\n                try:\n                    func()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    name = getattr(func, \"__qualname__\", func)\n                    logger.exception(\n                        \"Error calling %s in on_commit() during transaction (%s).\",\n                        name,\n                        e,\n                    )\n            else:\n                func()\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def execute_wrapper(self, wrapper):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a context manager under which the wrapper is applied to suitable\n        database query executions.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.execute_wrappers.append(wrapper)\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            self.execute_wrappers.pop()\n\n    def copy(self, alias=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a copy of this connection.\n\n        For tests that require two connections to the same database.\n        \"\"\"\n        settings_dict = copy.deepcopy(self.settings_dict)\n        if alias is None:\n            alias = self.alias\n        return type(self)(settings_dict, alias)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":246,"oldLines":7,"newStart":246,"newLines":7,"lines":["         # Return an\n        identifier for the savepoint that will be used for the subsequent\n        rollback or commit. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\n        tid = str(thread_ident).replace(\"-\", \"\")\n\n        self.savepoint_state += 1\n        sid = \"s%s_x%d\" % (tid, self.savepoint_state)\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint(sid)\n\n        return sid\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n        \"\"\"\n        Roll back to a savepoint. To avoid this buggy behavior and to actually enter a new\n        transaction, an explicit BEGIN is required.   \"The rollback flag doesn't work outside of an 'atomic' block.\"\n            )\n        return self.needs_rollback\n\n    def set_rollback(self, rollback):\n        \"\"\"\n        Set or unset the active.\"\"\"\n        if self.in_atomic_block:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"This is forbidden when an 'atomic' block is active.\"\n            )\n\n    def You can't \"\n                \"execute queries until the end of the 'atomic' block.\"\n            ) from self.rollback_exc\n\n    # ##### Foreign key constraints checks handling #####\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def constraint_checks_disabled(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Disable foreign key constraint checking.\n        \"\"\"\n        disabled = self.disable_constraint_checking()\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            if disabled:\n                self.enable_constraint_checking()\n\n    def disable_constraint_checking(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Backends can implement as needed to temporarily disable foreign key\n        constraint checking. Raise an exception if the validation fails.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not (self.allow_thread_sharing or self._thread_ident == _thread.get_ident()):\n            raise DatabaseError(\n                \"DatabaseWrapper objects created in a \"\n                \"thread can only be used in that same thread. Transaction in progress; save for execution on commit.\n            self.run_on_commit.append((set(self.savepoint_ids), func, robust))\n        elif not self.get_autocommit():\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"on_commit() cannot be used in manual transaction management\"\n            )\n        else:\n            # database version isn't supported by this\n        version of Django.\n        \"\"\"\n        if (\n            self.features.minimum_database_version is not None\n            and self.get_database_version() < self.features.minimum_database_version\n        ):\n            db_version = \".\".join(map(str, self.get_database_version()))\n            min_db_version = \"method\"\n        )\n\n    def get_new_connection(self, conn_params):\n        \"\"\"Open a connection to the database.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError(\n            \"subclasses of     \"\"\"Create a cursor. Assume that a connection is established.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError(\n            \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a create_cursor() method\"\n        )\n\n    # ##### Backend-specific methods for creating connections #####\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def connect(self):\n        \"\"\"Connect to the database. Assume that the connection is closed.\"\"\"\n        # Check for invalid configurations.\n        self.check_settings()\n        # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\n        self.in_atomic_block = False\n        self.savepoint_ids = []\n        self.atomic_blocks = []\n        self.needs_rollback = False\n        # Reset parameters defining when to close/health-check the connection.\n        self.health_check_enabled = self.settings_dict[\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\"]\n        max_age = self.settings_dict[\"CONN_MAX_AGE\"]\n        self.close_at = time.monotonic() if max_age == 0 else (None if max_age is None else time.monotonic() + max_age)\n        self.closed_in_transaction = False\n        self.errors_occurred = False\n        # New connections are healthy.\n        self.health_check_done = True\n        # Establish the connection\n        conn_params = self.get_connection_params()\n        self.connection = self.get_new_connection(conn_params)\n        self.set_autocommit(self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"])\n        self.init_connection_state()\n        connection_created.send(sender=self.__class__, connection=self)\n\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n    def check_settings(self):\n        if self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"] is not None and not settings.USE_TZ:\n            raise ImproperlyConfigured(\n                \"Connection '%s' cannot set TIME_ZONE because USE_TZ is False.\"\n                % self.alias\n            )\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def ensure_connection(self):\n        \"\"\"Guarantee that a connection to the database is established.\"\"\"\n        if self.connection is None:\n            if self.in_atomic_block and self.closed_in_transaction:\n                raise ProgrammingError(\n                    \"Cannot open a new connection in an atomic block.\"\n                )\n            with self.wrap_database_errors:\n                self.connect()\n\n    # ##### Generic wrappers for PEP-249 connection methods #####\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def cursor(self):\n        \"\"\"Create a cursor, opening a connection if necessary.\"\"\"\n        return self._cursor()\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def commit(self):\n        \"\"\"Commit a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        self._commit()\n        # A successful commit means that the database connection works.\n        self.errors_occurred = False\n        self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = True\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def rollback(self):\n        \"\"\"Roll back a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        self._rollback()\n        # A successful rollback means that the database connection works.\n        self.errors_occurred = False\n        self.needs_rollback = False\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close the connection to the database.\"\"\"\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n        # Don't call validate_no_atomic_block() to avoid making it difficult\n        # to get rid of a connection in an invalid state. The next connect()\n        # will reset the transaction state anyway.\n        if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\n            return\n        try:\n            self._close()\n        finally:\n            if self.in_atomic_block:\n                self.closed_in_transaction = True\n                self.needs_rollback = True\n            else:\n                self.connection = None\n\n    # ##### Backend-specific savepoint management methods #####\n\n    def _savepoint(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_create_sql(sid))\n\n    def _savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_rollback_sql(sid))\n\n    def _savepoint_commit(self, sid):\n        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_commit_sql(sid))\n\n    def _savepoint_allowed(self):\n        # Savepoints cannot be created outside a transaction\n        return self.features.uses_savepoints and not self.get_autocommit()\n\n    # ##### Generic savepoint management methods #####\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a savepoint inside the current transaction. Return an\n        identifier for the savepoint that will be used for the subsequent\n        rollback or commit. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\n        tid = str(thread_ident).replace(\"-\", \"\")\n\n        self.savepoint_state += 1\n        sid = \"s%s_x%d\" % (tid, self.savepoint_state)\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint(sid)\n\n        return sid\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n        \"\"\"\n        Roll back to a savepoint. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint_rollback(sid)\n\n        # Remove any callbacks registered while this savepoint was active.\n        self.run_on_commit = [\n            (sids, func, robust)\n            for (sids, func, robust) in self.run_on_commit\n            if sid not in sids\n        ]\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint_commit(self, sid):\n        \"\"\"\n        Release a savepoint. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint_commit(sid)\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def clean_savepoints(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Reset the counter used to generate unique savepoint ids in this thread.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.savepoint_state = 0\n\n    # ##### Backend-specific transaction management methods #####\n\n    def _set_autocommit(self, autocommit):\n        \"\"\"\n        Backend-specific implementation to enable or disable autocommit.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError(\n            \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a _set_autocommit() method\"\n        )\n\n    # ##### Generic transaction management methods #####\n\n    def get_autocommit(self):\n        \"\"\"Get the autocommit state.\"\"\"\n        self.ensure_connection()\n        return self.autocommit\n\n    def set_autocommit(\n        self, autocommit, force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=False\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Enable or disable autocommit.\n\n        The usual way to start a transaction is to turn autocommit off.\n        SQLite does not properly start a transaction when disabling\n        autocommit. To avoid this buggy behavior and to actually enter a new\n        transaction, an explicit BEGIN is required. Using\n        force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=True will issue an\n        explicit BEGIN with SQLite. This option will be ignored for other\n        backends.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n        self.close_if_health_check_failed()\n        self.ensure_connection()\n\n self._set_autocommit(autocommit)\n        else:\n            with debug_transaction(self, \"BEGIN\"):\n                self._set_autocommit(autocommit)\n        self.autocommit = autocommit\n\n validate_no_broken_transaction(self):\n        if self.needs_rollback:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"An error occurred in the current transaction. You can't \"\n                \"execute queries until the end of the 'atomic' block.\"\n            ) from self.rollback_exc\n\n    # ##### Foreign key constraints checks handling #####\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def constraint_checks_disabled(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Disable foreign key constraint checking.\n        \"\"\"\n        disabled = self.disable_constraint_checking()\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            if disabled:\n                self.enable_constraint_checking()\n\n    def disable_constraint_checking(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Backends can implement as needed to temporarily disable foreign key\n        constraint checking. Should raise an\n        IntegrityError if any invalid foreign key references are encountered.\n        \"\"\"\n        pass\n\n    # ##### Connection termination handling #####\n\n    def is_usable(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test if the database connection is usable.\n\n        This method may assume that self.connection is not None.\n\n        Actual implementations should take care not to raise exceptions\n        as that may prevent Django from recycling unusable connections.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError(\n            \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require an is_usable() method\"\n        )\n\n    def close_if_health_check_failed(self):\n        \"\"\"Close existing connection if it fails a health check.\"\"\"\n        if (\n            self.connection is None\n            or not self.health_check_enabled\n            or self.health_check_done\n        ):\n            return\n\n        if not self.is_usable():\n            self.close()\n        self.health_check_done = True\n\n    def close_if_unusable_or_obsolete(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Close the current connection if unrecoverable errors have occurred\n        or if it outlived its maximum age.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.connection is not None:\n            self.health_check_done = False\n            # Transaction in progress; save for execution on commit.\n            self.run_on_commit.append((set(self.savepoint_ids), func, robust))\n        elif not self.get_autocommit():\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"on_commit() cannot be used in manual transaction management\"\n            )\n        else:\n            # {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01VtSs4D9W3NG3xPnBR2LPPL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Wait, I just changed `>=` to `>`, but I'm not confident this is the right fix. Let me undo this and think more carefully.\n\nActually, let me re-examine the problem statement one more time. It says: \"However, monitoring shows that connections are being torn down and re-established after every request cycle, defeating the purpose of persistent connections entirely.\"\n\n\"After every request cycle\" - this could mean the connection is closed at the end of the request and reopened at the start of the next request. If that's happening for persistent connections, it suggests they're not staying open.\n\nLooking at the code flow:\n1. `connect()` is called, setting `close_at`\n2. At the end of the request, `close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()` is called\n3. If `close_at <= current_time`, the connection is closed\n4. On the next request, if the connection is None, a new one is created\n\nFor persistent connections (`max_age = None`), `close_at` should be None, so step 3 should not close the connection.UNLESS... what if the condition is being checked WRONG?\n\nLike, what if the operator in the comparison is `>=` when it should be `==`? Then persistent connections would only be closed if the time EXACTLY matches, which shouldn't happen...no, that doesn't make sense.OR what if the operator should be `<` instead of `>`? Then the connection would be closed if the current time is BEFORE the close time, which is backwards...\n\nACTUALLY! HOLD ON! Return an\n        identifier for the savepoint that will be used for the subsequent\n        rollback or commit. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n            return\n\n        thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\n        tid = str(thread_ident).replace(\"-\", \"\")\n\n        self.savepoint_state += 1\n        sid = \"s%s_x%d\" % (tid, self.savepoint_state)\n\n        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n        self._savepoint(sid)\n\n        return sid\n\n    @async_unsafe\n    def savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n        \"\"\"\n        Roll back to a savepoint. To avoid this buggy behavior and to actually enter a new\n        transaction, an explicit BEGIN is required. Using\n        force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=True will issue an\n        explicit BEGIN with SQLite.   \"The rollback flag doesn't work outside of an 'atomic' block.\"\n            )\n        return self.needs_rollback\n\n    def set_rollback(self, rollback):\n        \"\"\"\n        Set or unset the active.\"\"\"\n        if self.in_atomic_block:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"This is forbidden when an 'atomic' block is active.\"\n            )\n\n    def validate_no_broken_transaction(self):\n        if self.needs_rollback:\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"An error occurred in the current transaction. You can't \"\n                \"execute queries until the end of the 'atomic' block.\"\n            ) from self.rollback_exc\n\n    # ##### Foreign key constraints checks handling #####\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def constraint_checks_disabled(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Disable foreign key constraint checking.\n        \"\"\"\n        disabled = self.disable_constraint_checking()\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            if disabled:\n                self.enable_constraint_checking()\n\n    def disable_constraint_checking(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Backends can implement as needed to temporarily disable foreign key\n        constraint checking. If an exception other than DataError or IntegrityError occurred\n            # since the last commit / rollback, check if the connection works.\n            if self.errors_occurred:\n                if self.is_usable():\n                    self.errors_occurred = False\n                    self.health_check_done = True\n                else:\n                    self.close()\n                    return\n\n            if self.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() > self.close_at:\n                self.close()\n                return\n\n    # ##### Thread safety handling #####\n\n    @property\n    def allow_thread_sharing(self):\n        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n            return self._thread_sharing_count > 0\n\n    def inc_thread_sharing(self):\n        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n            self._thread_sharing_count += 1\n\n    def dec_thread_sharing(self):\n        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n            if self._thread_sharing_count <= 0:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"Cannot decrement the thread sharing count below zero.\"\n                )\n            self._thread_sharing_count -= 1\n\n    def validate_thread_sharing(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate that the connection isn't accessed by another thread than the\n        one which originally created it, unless the connection was explicitly\n        authorized to be shared between threads (via the `inc_thread_sharing()`\n        method). Raise an exception if the validation fails.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not (self.allow_thread_sharing or self._thread_ident == _thread.get_ident()):\n            raise DatabaseError(\n                \"DatabaseWrapper objects created in a \"\n                \"thread can only be used in that same thread. The object \"\n                \"with alias '%s' was created in thread id %s and this is \"\n                \"thread id %s.\" % (self.alias, self._thread_ident, _thread.get_ident())\n            )\n\n    #         yield cursor\n        finally:\n            conn.close()\n\n    def schema_editor(self, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a new instance of this backend's SchemaEditor.\n be a callable.\")\n        if self.in_atomic_block:\n            # Transaction in progress; save for execution on commit.\n            self.run_on_commit.append((set(self.savepoint_ids), func, robust))\n        elif not self.get_autocommit():\n            raise TransactionManagementError(\n                \"on_commit() cannot be used in manual transaction management\"\n            )\n except Exception as e:\n                    name = getattr(func, \"__qualname__\", func)\n                    logger.exception(\"Error calling %s in on_commit() (%s).\", name, e)\n            else:\n
